title: Web Server Process Spawning Shell with Injection Characters (Linux)
description:
Detects a web application runtime (Python, Node, Java, PHP) spawning a shell
process (bash, sh, dash) with command-line arguments containing injection
characters (semicolons, pipes, backticks). This indicates successful Remote
Code Execution (RCE) via a command injection vulnerability in the web app.
Covers MITRE ATT&CK T1059 (Command and Scripting Interpreter) and
T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application).
query: ('Log Source' = 'SOC Linux Syslog Logs' or 'Log Source' = 'Linux Secure Logs' or 'Log Source' = 'Linux Syslog Logs' or 'Log Source' = 'Linux Audit Logs') and (((msg like '*ppid=python*' or msg like '*ppid=python3*' or msg like '*ppid=node*' or msg like '*ppid=java*' or msg like '*ppid=php*' or msg like '*ppid=ruby*' or msg like '*ppid=uvicorn*' or msg like '*ppid=gunicorn*' or msg like '*ppid=nginx*' or msg like '*ppid=httpd*')) and ((msg like '*exe="/bin/bash"*' or msg like '*exe="/bin/sh"*' or msg like '*exe="/bin/dash"*' or msg like '*exe="/usr/bin/bash"*' or msg like '*exe="/usr/bin/sh"*')) and ((msg like '*;*' or msg like '*|*' or msg like '*&&*' or msg like '*`*' or msg like '*$(*' or msg like '*whoami*' or msg like '*/etc/passwd*' or msg like '*/etc/shadow*')))
falsepositives: Legitimate subprocess calls from web application deployment scripts
